commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `compat.systemPromptPlacement` for OpenAI Responses models so configured proxies can move `context.systemPrompt` from `input` into top-level `instructions`, with docs and targeted tests.

PR surface: Source +27, Tests +57, Docs +22. Total +106 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main places non-Codex OpenAI Responses system prompts in `input` through `convertResponsesMessages`, so a strict proxy rejecting system/developer input items would still fail. I did not run a live proxy reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor proof and maintainer review of the new config surface; automation should not take over while the real-behavior proof gate is missing.

target.title: [codex] support Responses system prompts in instructions
target.body:
## Summary

Add an OpenAI Responses compatibility switch for providers that reject `system` or `developer` messages inside the Responses `input` array.

When a configured Responses model sets `compat.systemPromptPlacement: "instructions"`, OpenClaw now sends `context.systemPrompt` as top-level `instructions` and keeps `input` to user/tool/replay items. The default remains unchanged: normal `openai-responses` models still place the system prompt in `input`, while Codex Responses keeps its existing top-level instructions behavior.

This covers OpenAI-compatible Responses gateways that expose the Responses API but enforce a stricter request schema and return errors such as `400 System messages are not allowed`.

## What changed

- Adds `compat.systemPromptPlacement?: "input" | "instructions"` to OpenAI Responses compat metadata.
- Wires the switch through TypeScript types, runtime model registry schema, strict config Zod schema, and request construction.
- Adds regression coverage for a local Responses-compatible provider using top-level `instructions`.
- Documents the config in the OpenAI provider proxy/compatible route notes.

## Verification

- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/zod-schema.models.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check packages/llm-core/src/types.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts src/config/types.models.ts src/config/zod-schema.core.ts src/config/zod-schema.models.test.ts src/agents/sessions/model-registry.ts src/llm/providers/openai-responses.ts`

## Notes

`pnpm check:changed --staged` could not run locally because the configured Crabbox/Testbox wrapper failed its binary sanity check before starting checks. The targeted local tests and core type check above passed.
target.author: skingford
